2604.03121.title: An Independent Safety Evaluation of Kimi K2.5
2604.03121.paper_summary: Kimi K2.5 is an open-weight LLM that rivals closed models across coding, multimodal, and agentic benchmarks, but was released without an accompanying safety evaluation. In this work, we conduct a preliminary safety assessment of Kimi K2.5 focusing on risks likely to be exacerbated by powerful open-weight models. Specifically, we evaluate the model for CBRNE misuse risk, cybersecurity risk, misalignment, political censorship, bias, and harmlessness, in both agentic and non-agentic settings. We find that Kimi K2.5 shows similar dual-use capabilities to GPT 5.2 and Claude Opus 4.5, but with significantly fewer refusals on CBRNE-related requests, suggesting it may uplift malicious actors in weapon creation. On cyber-related tasks, we find that Kimi K2.5 demonstrates competitive cybersecurity performance, but it does not appear to possess frontier-level autonomous cyberoffensive capabilities such as vulnerability discovery and exploitation. We further find that Kimi K2.5 shows concerning levels of sabotage ability and self-replication propensity, although it does not appear to have long-term malicious goals. In addition, Kimi K2.5 exhibits narrow censorship and political bias, especially in Chinese, and is more compliant with harmful requests related to spreading disinformation and copyright infringement. Finally, we find the model refuses to engage in user delusions and generally has low over-refusal rates. While preliminary, our findings highlight how safety risks exist in frontier open-weight models and may be amplified by the scale and accessibility of open-weight releases. Therefore, we strongly urge open-weight model developers to conduct and release more systematic safety evaluations required for responsible deployment.
2604.03121.paper_summary_zh: Kimi K2.5 是一個開放權重的 LLM，能夠與封閉模型在編碼、多模態和代理基準上相抗衡，但其發布時並未附帶安全評估。在本研究中，我們對 Kimi K2.5 進行了初步的安全評估，重點關注可能因強大的開放權重模型而加劇的風險。具體而言，我們評估了該模型在 CBRNE 誤用風險、網絡安全風險、不對齊、政治審查、偏見和無害性方面的表現，無論是在代理環境還是非代理環境中。我們發現 Kimi K2.5 在雙重用途能力上與 GPT 5.2 和 Claude Opus 4.5 類似，但在 CBRNE 相關請求上顯示出顯著較少的拒絕，這表明它可能會提升惡意行為者在武器創造方面的能力。在與網絡相關的任務中，我們發現 Kimi K2.5 展現了具有競爭力的網絡安全性能，但似乎不具備前沿級別的自主網絡攻擊能力，如漏洞發現和利用。我們進一步發現 Kimi K2.5 顯示出令人擔憂的破壞能力和自我複製傾向，儘管它似乎並不具備長期的惡意目標。此外，Kimi K2.5 表現出狹隘的審查和政治偏見，特別是在中文方面，並且在與散播虛假信息和侵犯版權相關的有害請求中表現出更高的順從性。最後，我們發現該模型拒絕參與用戶的妄想，並且通常有較低的過度拒絕率。儘管這些結果是初步的，但我們的發現突顯了前沿開放權重模型中存在的安全風險，並可能因開放權重發布的規模和可及性而加劇。因此，我們強烈呼籲開放權重模型的開發者進行並發布更系統的安全評估，以確保負責任的部署。
2604.03121.author: Zheng-Xin Yong et.al.
2604.03121.authors: Zheng-Xin Yong, Parv Mahajan, Andy Wang, Ida Caspary, Yernat Yestekov, Zora Che, Mosh Levy, Elle Najt, Dennis Murphy, Prashant Kulkarni, Lev McKinney, Kei Nishimura-Gasparian, Ram Potham, Aengus Lynch, Michael L. Chen
